name: Lumoo
tagline: AI content creation platform for fashion and retail
narrative_short: AI content platform replacing fashion photoshoots with virtual try-on and brand modelling; €800K ARR in 9 months serving 15+ Nordic brands including Gant and Brothers.
narrative_long: Lumoo is an AI-native content studio for fashion and retail brands, built entirely on Lovable by two non-technical co-founders in Stockholm. The product replaces parts of the traditional photoshoot pipeline — virtual try-on, automated brand modelling, localized product imagery — and explicitly targets the 'returns problem' in e-commerce fashion. Within nine months of launch Lumoo was reportedly at €800k ARR with more than 15 Nordic brand customers (Gant, Brothers, Zoovillage, AWNR Group, Johnels are named publicly). Henrik Skagerlind Fasth has said the product would have taken 'a minimum of two years to develop in another way.' In December 2025 Lovable CEO Anton Osika led a ~€550k angel round — a rare case of Lovable's founder personally backing a company built on his platform, which Tech.eu flagged as a signal of where the ecosystem is going. Lumoo is the current 'poster child' European Lovable-native scaleup: real enterprise customers, real ARR, no technical founders, shipped fast.

sources.excerpt: This would have taken a minimum of two years to develop in another way. And then we're not even talking about the dramatic difference in cost.
Lumoo, an AI fashion platform with €800k ARR in nine months.
Lovable-built Lumoo gets funding from Lovable CEO.

notes: Sources disagree on ARR: blog cites €700K, Series B post cites €800K. Stored lower bound in notes; used €800K from press.

name: QuickTables
narrative_short: Two 20-year-olds in Malmö quit their jobs with 60 days of runway and built a restaurant growth tool on Lovable — €90K ARR in two months.
narrative_long: QuickTables is an all-in-one restaurant growth tool — mobile-friendly sites, direct ordering, SMS marketing, loyalty — built in Malmö by two 20-year-old founders, Kevin Sandmark and Jaleel Miles, on a 60-day runway. Local Swedish press (Øresund Startups) reported €90K ARR within two months of launch; Lovable's own Series B post later cited 'over $100K/year' and framed them as proof that young non-technical founders can ship B2B SaaS without raising. The founders quit their jobs to commit full-time before revenue existed — a much less polished but very concrete 'Lovable enabled this' story than the Lumoo narrative. Note: Lovable's Series B post names the co-founder pair as 'Jaleel and Hussein' which conflicts with the local Swedish reporting; we've stored the lower-confidence names from press and flagged for follow-up.

sources.excerpt: These Swedish 20-year-olds just reached €90k ARR with a Lovable-built app they built in 2 months.
QuickTables — Jaleel and Hussein — over $100K/year.

notes: Founder name conflict — Swedish press says Kevin Sandmark + Jaleel Miles; Lovable blog says Jaleel + Hussein. Needs verification.

name: Brickwise
tagline: AI property manager for landlords and estate agents
narrative_short: AI property manager diagnosing maintenance issues from tenant photos and scheduling contractors; MVP built on Lovable with no code written, into YC F25 with $500K check.
narrative_long: Brickwise is an AI property manager aimed at landlords and estate agents — tenants submit an issue with a photo, the AI diagnoses the problem, schedules a contractor, and handles payment. The MVP was built entirely on Lovable with no code written, which became the pitch angle when co-founders Ismail Jeilani and Elias Kassell applied to Y Combinator's F25 batch. They got in with a $500k YC check and went on to raise more than $3M total. Sifted reported a >$1M cARR enterprise contract in pipeline, with 10+ customers spanning London, the US and Dubai. Ismail's prior company LiveLink AI raised $3M and ran a property business to $500K ARR; Elias was an early engineer at Dataform (YC W18, acquired by Google) — so the 'non-technical vibe-coded MVP' headline is partly real, partly PR: there's a senior engineer on the team who chose Lovable for speed rather than out of necessity. Brickwise is the strongest YC/VC-validated example of a Lovable-born European company.

sources.excerpt: won a spot in Y Combinator's winter batch, bagging $500k of investment without writing a single line of code
Brickwise — AI property manager for the rental market.
Brickwise (YC-backed property management, $500K funding).

name: Stardust
tagline: Evolution marketing — AI that autonomously writes, designs and tests ad creative
narrative_short: Won Lovable's global hackathon out of 2,000 teams, pre-sold to ~€100K ARR before writing code, then raised €1.7M pre-seed with Anton Osika angel-investing.
narrative_long: Stardust calls itself 'evolution marketing' — an AI that analyzes every frame, word and pixel of an ad, learns what persuades, and autonomously writes and designs new variants. The company won Lovable's global AI hackathon in April 2025 (beating 2,000 teams and taking a $10K prize) and reportedly pre-sold the product to around €100K ARR before writing any code. In November 2025 Stardust raised €1.7M pre-seed led by Pitchdrive with Anton Osika angel-investing alongside Jonas Dhaenens (Team.Blue), Further Than Capital and SSE Ventures. It is one of the first pure Lovable-hackathon-to-funded-company stories, and — like Lumoo — has Lovable's CEO on its cap table, which both validates and biases the signal.

sources.excerpt: €1.7M pre-seed led by Pitchdrive; Anton Osika angel-invested.
I'm officially an angel investor in a software startup built with Lovable. The team is cracked, and the product is impressive – they're going to go far.

notes: Philip Stjernstoft may be a fourth co-founder per Pitchdrive.

name: Tribbai
tagline: Natural collaborative dialogues between multiple AI roles
narrative_short: Portuguese solo founder building a platform where multiple AI 'roles' converse with each other; 65 on waitlist after 4 months of development on Lovable.
narrative_long: Tribbai is Sergio Gonçalves's solo attempt at a pattern that several builders are circling: a shared space where multiple AI personas converse, disagree and collaborate in public. He built it on Lovable over four months and had 65 people on the waitlist at Lovable's blog feature time in January 2025 — a modestly early story included here because it is a clean, non-winner-yet European example of how the platform lowers the cost of 'let me try this idea' for non-technical founders.

sources.excerpt: Sergio is crafting Tribbai by using Lovable — 65 on waitlist.

name: PrintPigeon
tagline: Upload a document, we print and mail it
narrative_short: Greek founder in London shipped a print-and-mail service in 3 days for $38 of Lovable credits; ~half the users turned out to be expats without printer access.
narrative_long: PrintPigeon is a simple but instructive story: a Greek founder in London, Yannis Karagiannidis, noticed that expats and remote workers frequently need to mail a physical letter — a signed form, a visa document — and have no printer. He shipped a web service where you upload a PDF, enter an address, and PrintPigeon prints and mails it. Build time: three days. Build cost: $38 in Lovable credits. Initial marketing: £100 of Google ads. The user base skewed ~50% to expats, which became the positioning. Yannis later joined Lovable as VP of Growth, so the product is now a side project — meaning PrintPigeon is both a clean 'Lovable-native European product' case and a cautionary note about how founder-marketed case studies can lose their independent founder after the fact.

sources.excerpt: Built in 3 days; dev cost $38 in Lovable credits; nearly 50% of my users turned out to be expats.

notes: Yannis joined Lovable as VP of Growth after this feature — product is now a side project.

name: Elora Health
narrative_short: Non-technical UK founder shipped a personalised health challenge app on Lovable in a month with 2 paying customers at launch.
narrative_long: Tom Skyrme had no coding background and a history of previous health ventures (Animus Health, Affinity Nutrition). He used Lovable to build Elora Health — an app that generates personalised challenges across nutrition, workouts and wellness — from scratch in a month. At Lovable's feature time he had two paying customers. This is exactly the 'T2 early traction' archetype Lovable is courting: a non-technical founder with domain knowledge who can now ship a real product over a weekend and iterate.

sources.excerpt: Right now, we have two paying customers. It's a start, and I'm excited to keep improving the product.

name: Backchannel
tagline: Job search guidance for mid-career operators
narrative_short: Non-technical founder built a job search guidance tool on Lovable; $7-8K lifetime from zero promotion before joining Lovable as Growth.
narrative_long: Mindaugas Petrutis is a Lithuanian-Irish non-technical founder with a hospitality and design-recruiting background. Backchannel is a job-search guidance tool he built on Lovable in desperation after trying several no-code paths — it was his first real product. Revenue is small ($7-8K lifetime, via a Stripe link he generated in minutes) but the story matters for the dataset because it's a clean T2 'early traction, non-technical, built on Lovable' datapoint with founder-verified numbers. He later co-founded Coho (peer groups for designers/engineers/data scientists) and was recruited into Lovable itself as a growth hire. Backchannel continues to generate a few Stripe pings a week with zero promotion.

sources.excerpt: I didn't even have a pricing model. I just generated a quick Stripe link and sent it over.

notes: Founder now at Lovable as Growth hire. Also founded Coho (joincoho.com).

name: Lifetoon
tagline: Turn life moments into illustrated comics with AI
narrative_short: Romanian team of 5 launched a 'life moments → comics' AI product in 6 weeks using Lovable for prototyping; 100+ users in the first 24 hours.
narrative_long: Lifetoon is a Romanian consumer-AI product that turns personal stories into illustrated comic strips. The team of five used Lovable for rapid prototyping — raw story descriptions and wireframes became an interactive prototype in under an hour — though the production stack isn't fully documented. Built in six weeks, launched in May 2025 with more than 100 users in the first 24 hours. The The Recursive article is the main source. Classified as `partial` confidence because the production stack beyond the Lovable prototype isn't confirmed.

sources.excerpt: raw story descriptions and crude wireframes into Lovable, which spun up an interactive prototype in under an hour.

name: GoodOmen
narrative_short: UK team built a video-gen platform and an AI journalist avatar in 20 hours at Project Lovable hackathon; took €40K in prize and acquisition on the spot.
narrative_long: GoodOmen is a London team of three led by Aaron Jones (also co-founder of Yepic AI) that built two products in 20 hours during the Project Lovable x Project Europe hackathon in Stockholm in August 2025: a video-generation platform and an AI journalist tool that writes articles from interviews at speed. They won the €20K hackathon prize, and Impact Loop — a Stockholm publication covering impact investing — acquired the journalism tool on the spot for another €20K. The team is continuing to develop the journalism product in partnership with Impact Loop. GoodOmen is included here as the highest-signal hackathon-to-revenue story in the European Lovable ecosystem.

sources.excerpt: Won €20K at Project Lovable x Project Europe hackathon; journalism tool acquired on-spot by Impact Loop for additional €20K.
Impact Loop's avatar idea wins at Lovable and Project Europe hackathon.

name: BnBicons
narrative_short: French developer advocate in Barcelona shipped an isometric icon generator on Lovable; crossed $2K total, 40K icons generated, 15K users — textbook indie Lovable build.
narrative_long: Nicolas Grenié (@picsoung) is a French developer advocate (ex-Typeform) based in Barcelona, active as a Lovable ambassador. BnBicons is his consumer-ish hit: a generator of Airbnb-style isometric icons that designers use for landing pages and decks. Numbers are modest — $2K lifetime revenue, 40K icons generated, 15K users — but he's unusually transparent about the figures on X with #buildinpublic, which makes BnBicons one of the cleanest 'self-reported T2 indie' datapoints in the European Lovable set. He has several other Lovable-built projects (ContractionTrack, Feature logo banner) which are captured in breadth.

sources.excerpt: Bnbicons earned 30€ today.
How Bnbicons.com turned an Airbnb design trend into a high-velocity startup.

name: Launchli
tagline: Distribution and launch automation for SaaS founders
narrative_short: 19-year-old Swedish founder built a distribution automation tool on Lovable; $146 MRR and 100+ users in 30 days from manual outreach.
narrative_long: Sebastian Mattsson (@seb_matts) is a 19-year-old solo founder in Sweden who built Launchli on Lovable — a tool for SaaS founders to automate their early distribution work (Reddit, X, LinkedIn, manual outreach). Public beta numbers in month one: $146 MRR and 100+ users, earned through exactly the kind of manual founder outreach the product tries to replace. Small numbers by design — included here because he's a rare European Lovable builder who publicly discloses real MRR figures, positioning himself explicitly as 'building the product was never the hard part, distributing it was.'

sources.excerpt: How Sebastian got his first 100 users from Reddit, X, LinkedIn and manual outreach — $146 MRR in 30 days.

name: Airweave
tagline: Open-source context retrieval for AI agents
narrative_short: Amsterdam-founded, YC X25 open-source context layer for LLM agents; first prototype built on Lovable before relocating to SF.
narrative_long: Airweave is a Dutch-founded open-source context retrieval layer for AI agents — you point it at your data sources and it keeps them queryable by LLMs. Lennert Jansen and Rauf Akdemir founded it in Amsterdam, built the first prototype on Lovable, and then relocated to San Francisco for YC's X25 batch in April 2025. They now report seven employees. Revenue isn't disclosed. Included here as a European-founded Lovable-prototyped company that followed the classic YC-to-SF path — a counterpoint to the 'stay in Europe' Lovable narrative.

sources.excerpt: Airweave (YC X25) — first prototype built on Lovable.
Airweave — open-source context for AI agents.

notes: Production stack may have moved off Lovable — hence partial confidence.

name: Imaginary Space
tagline: Agency building MVPs and internal tools on Lovable
narrative_short: UK-registered agency (founder now in Buenos Aires) tripled revenue in a year by delivering Lovable-built MVPs and internal tools to enterprise clients.
narrative_long: Imaginary Space is a UK-registered agency run by Harry Roper McKay, a frontend engineer who moved from Devon to Buenos Aires. The business is a pure Lovable-native consultancy: MVPs, internal tools, client portals — all delivered out of Lovable to enterprise clients. Harry says revenue tripled in the year after adopting Lovable, with six-figure contracts and a stated target of $1M ARR for 2025 (hit 20% of that in January alone, per the Lovable blog feature). Included here as the cleanest European 'Lovable as a service delivery engine' case — distinct from product startups.

sources.excerpt: I've never achieved this kind of revenue without a tool like Lovable.

notes: Agency/services business; UK HQ but founder resides in Argentina.

name: NHS Clinical Apps (Arun)
tagline: Healthcare workflow apps built by an NHS pharmacist
narrative_short: NHS pharmacist built 20+ healthcare workflow apps on Lovable and organised Clinical AI Hackathons across 7 UK locations with 300+ participants.
narrative_long: A London-based NHS pharmacist going by Arun built more than 20 healthcare workflow apps on Lovable — QOF Progress Tracker, Smart Blood Test Assistant, Patient Triage Navigator, among others — and organised Clinical AI Hackathons across seven UK locations with 300+ participants. No revenue; the apps are internal NHS tooling or freely shared. Included to represent the 'public-sector domain expert uses Lovable' archetype, which Lovable's own marketing highlights but rarely contextualises with real numbers.

sources.excerpt: Reimagining healthcare innovation inside the NHS — 20+ apps built, 7 Clinical AI Hackathons.

notes: Founder's full name not public in the blog.
